ABW: Which island lies just south of the Caribbean hurricane belt, where direct hurricane hits are rare enough that locals can usually name the handful that happened from memory?
AFG: Which country has changed its national flag more than 20 times since 1901, reportedly more than any other country in modern history — a reflection of just how many governments have risen and fallen there?
AGO: Which African country, despite being one of the continent's top oil producers, still imports most of its refined gasoline because it has only one working refinery?
AIA: Which island has no rivers, lakes, or streams at all, meaning every drop of fresh water comes from rain catchment or desalination?
ALA: Which autonomous territory belongs to Finland, though nearly everyone there speaks Swedish, and issues its own postage stamps and vehicle plates?
ALB: Which country built over 170,000 concrete bunkers under a Cold War-era regime for a population of under 3 million — more per capita than any other country?
AND: Which country has one of the highest life expectancies in the world, credited partly to its mountain air and highly active population?
ARE: Which country's police force patrols in a fleet that includes a Bugatti Veyron and a Lamborghini Aventador, marketed as the world's fastest police cars?
ARG: Which country's capital is home to 9 de Julio Avenue, often called the widest avenue on Earth — wide enough to take several traffic-light cycles to cross on foot?
ARM: Which country was the first in the world to adopt Christianity as a state religion, in the year 301 AD?
ASM: Which territory's residents are U.S. nationals but not U.S. citizens by birth — a unique legal status shared by no U.S. state?
ATA: Which continent has no native population, no time zone of its own, and no country situated there, being governed instead by an international treaty?
ATF: Which territory has no permanent residents at all, only rotating scientists stationed on its remote subantarctic islands?
ATG: Which country's tourism board famously claims '365 beaches, one for every day of the year' — a promotional line that's stuck for decades, even though the exact count depends on how you define a beach?
AUS: More than a third of which country's landmass lies within the tropics, even though most of its population lives along the temperate southeastern coast?
AUT: In which country was the Christmas carol 'Silent Night' written and first performed, in a small village church in 1818?
AZE: Which country's name is thought to mean 'land of fire,' inspired by natural gas seeps that have burned continuously from its hillsides for millennia?
BDI: Which country is one of the least electrified on Earth, where most of its population has never had access to grid electricity?
BEL: Which country has three official languages — Dutch, French, and German — despite being smaller than the U.S. state of Maryland?
BEN: Which country is considered the birthplace of Vodun, which remains an officially recognized religion practiced by a large share of the population?
BFA: Which country's name means 'land of upright/honest people,' adopted after independence to replace its colonial name, Upper Volta?
BGD: Which country is criss-crossed by hundreds of rivers and tributaries, giving it one of the densest river networks of any country on Earth — estimates of the total range from roughly 230 major rivers up to 700 once every tributary is counted?
BGR: In which country do people traditionally nod their head for 'no' and shake it side to side for 'yes' — the reverse of most of the world?
BHR: Which country's name means 'two seas' in Arabic, referring to natural underground layers of fresh and salt water once found around the island?
BHS: Which country's Exuma islands are home to a colony of wild pigs that swim out to greet passing boats, with no confirmed explanation for how they got there?
BIH: Which country's capital is nicknamed the 'Jerusalem of Europe' for its cluster of mosques, churches, and a synagogue all within a few blocks of each other?
BLM: Which island was a Swedish colony for nearly a century before France bought it back in 1878, its capital, Gustavia, still named for a Swedish king?
BLR: Nearly 40% of which country is covered in forest, home to some of the last European bison living in the wild?
BLZ: Which is the only country in Central America where English is the official language, a legacy of British colonial rule as British Honduras?
BMU: Which island isn't in the Caribbean at all, sitting alone in the North Atlantic over 1,000 km from the nearest Caribbean island?
BOL: Which country has two capitals — Sucre as the constitutional capital, and La Paz, the seat of government, sitting 3,640 meters up as the highest administrative capital in the world?
BRA: Which country shares a border with every other South American country except Chile and Ecuador?
BRB: Which country became the world's newest republic in 2021, removing the British monarch as head of state after nearly 400 years?
BRN: Which country's sultan has a personal car collection reportedly numbering in the thousands, funded by vast oil and gas reserves?
BTN: Which country measures national progress with a 'Gross National Happiness' index instead of GDP, and is one of the only carbon-negative countries on Earth?
BWA: Which country is home to more elephants than any other country on Earth, with a population estimated well over 100,000?
CAF: Which country sits almost exactly at the geographic center of the African continent?
CAN: Which country is home to roughly two million lakes — more than any other country on Earth, covering almost 9% of its total land area?
CHE: Which country has enough nuclear fallout shelters to house more than 100% of its population?
CHL: Which country stretches over 4,300 km from north to south but averages only about 175 km wide, making it one of the longest, thinnest countries on Earth?
CHN: Which country spans five geographic time zones but uses only one official time zone nationwide?
CIV: Which country's official capital is Yamoussoukro, even though nearly every embassy and most government activity remains in the larger city of Abidjan?
CMR: Which country is nicknamed 'Africa in miniature' because it contains nearly every major climate and landscape on the continent, from desert to rainforest to snow-capped peaks?
COD: Which country's principal river is the deepest in the world, plunging to depths of over 220 meters in places?
COG: Which country's capital sits directly across the river from the capital of its similarly-named neighbor, making them the closest two capital cities to each other in the world?
COK: Which country is self-governing in free association with New Zealand, with residents holding New Zealand citizenship despite the country issuing its own currency and stamps?
COL: Which is the only South American country with coastlines on both the Pacific Ocean and the Caribbean Sea?
COM: Which island nation is one of the world's largest producers of ylang-ylang, a flower essential to many high-end perfumes, earning it the nickname 'Perfume Islands'?
CPV: Which island nation was uninhabited until Portuguese sailors settled it in the 15th century, later making it a key hub in the transatlantic slave trade?
CRI: Which country abolished its military entirely in 1948 and has remained one of the most stable democracies in Latin America ever since?
CUB: Which country's classic American cars from the 1950s are still driven daily, kept running for decades by mechanics who can't legally import new parts?
CUW: Which island gave its name to the blue liqueur originally made from the dried peels of the bitter Laraha orange that grows almost nowhere else?
CYM: Which territory has more registered companies than people, thanks to its status as one of the world's largest offshore financial centers?
CYP: Which country has been divided since 1974 by a UN buffer zone, leaving its capital as the world's last divided capital city?
CZE: Which country has the highest per-capita beer consumption in the world, with brewing traditions that trace back centuries to towns like Pilsen?
DEU: Which country is home to more than 1,500 registered types of sausage, and had its bread culture — with over 3,000 documented recipes — recognized by UNESCO as Intangible Cultural Heritage in 2014?
DJI: Which country hosts military bases from the U.S., France, China, and Japan simultaneously, all within a country smaller than Massachusetts?
DMA: Which country, nicknamed the 'Nature Island,' is popularly said to have 365 rivers — one for every day of the year — though the exact number depends on how small a stream has to be to count?
DNK: Which country's Lego bricks are precise enough that a brick made in 1958 still fits perfectly with one made today?
DOM: Which country shares the island of Hispaniola with its neighbor, the only island in the world divided between two independent nations for centuries?
DZA: Which is Africa's largest country by land area, with over 80% of it covered by the Sahara Desert?
ECU: Because of the equatorial bulge, the summit of which country's Mount Chimborazo is the point on Earth's surface farthest from the planet's center — farther out than Everest's peak?
EGY: In which country does the Great Pyramid stand, having been the tallest human-made structure on Earth for almost 3,800 years — the longest such reign of any building in history?
ERI: Which country's capital is a UNESCO World Heritage Site nicknamed 'Africa's Art Deco city' for its remarkably preserved 1930s modernist architecture?
ESH: Which territory is Africa's last major unresolved colonial-era territorial dispute, claimed by both Morocco and the Sahrawi independence movement?
ESP: Which is the only country with land on both mainland Europe and mainland Africa, via its cities of Ceuta and Melilla?
EST: Which country was the first to allow nationwide online voting and remains one of the most digitally governed societies on Earth?
ETH: Which country runs on its own calendar, currently several years behind the Gregorian calendar, and marks the new year in September?
FIN: Which country has more saunas than cars, with an estimated one sauna for roughly every two people?
FJI: Which country is made up of over 330 islands, of which only about a third are permanently inhabited?
FLK: Which territory is home to roughly 500,000 sheep, outnumbering the human population by more than 130 to 1?
FRA: Which is the most visited country in the world, welcoming more international tourists annually than any other nation?
FRO: Which territory has more sheep than people, its name literally translating to 'Sheep Islands'?
FSM: Which country is spread across more than 600 islands over an expanse of Pacific roughly the size of the continental United States?
GAB: Which country set aside roughly 11% of its land as national parkland in a single stroke in 2002, one of the highest shares of protected rainforest in the world?
GBR: 'Big Ben' — technically just the bell inside a clock tower officially named Elizabeth Tower — stands in which country?
GEO: Which country claims to be the birthplace of wine, with archaeological evidence of winemaking dating back roughly 8,000 years?
GGY: Which territory issues its own banknotes and coins, even though it uses the British pound and isn't part of the United Kingdom or the EU?
GHA: Which was the first sub-Saharan African country to gain independence from colonial rule, in 1957?
GIN: Which country holds some of the world's largest known reserves of bauxite, the ore used to produce aluminum?
GMB: Which is mainland Africa's smallest country, a narrow strip of land following its namesake river and almost entirely surrounded by Senegal?
GNB: Which country's Bijagos Archipelago is one of the few places in the world where hippos are known to swim in the open ocean between islands?
GNQ: Which is the only African country where Spanish is an official language, a legacy of Spanish colonial rule?
GRC: In which country is discovering ancient ruins during construction common enough that a metro line, apartment building, or airport project can be delayed for years while archaeologists excavate the site first?
GRD: Which country is nicknamed the 'Island of Spice' for being one of the world's largest producers of nutmeg, which even appears on its national flag?
GRL: Which is the world's largest island, over 80% of it permanently covered by an ice sheet?
GTM: Which country is home to over 30 volcanoes, several still active, more than any other Central American country?
GUM: Which territory is often called 'where America's day begins,' since it sits just west of the international date line among the first U.S. territories to greet each new day?
GUY: Which is the only English-speaking country in South America, a legacy of British colonial rule as British Guiana?
HKG: Which territory has more skyscrapers than any other city in the world, packing thousands of tall buildings into a famously mountainous, land-scarce territory?
HMD: Which remote territory, rarely visited more than once a decade, is home to Australia's only active volcano, nicknamed Big Ben?
HND: Which country is home to the second-largest barrier reef system in the world, part of the Mesoamerican Reef along its Caribbean coast?
HRV: The necktie traces back to the scarves worn by which country's mercenaries in 17th-century France, whose name gave us the word 'cravat'?
HTI: Which country became the first in the world founded by a successful slave revolt, declaring independence from France in 1804?
HUN: The Rubik's Cube was invented in 1974 by Erno Rubik, an architecture professor in the capital of which country, originally as a teaching tool for spatial reasoning?
IDN: Which is the largest archipelago nation on Earth, having officially registered 16,056 islands with the United Nations in 2017?
IMN: Which territory's parliament, Tynwald, has met continuously since 979 AD, making it one of the oldest continuous parliaments in the world?
IND: Which country has more than 20 official languages and hundreds more spoken regionally, yet designates no single language as 'national' in its constitution?
INDIAN-OCEAN-TER: Which French-administered island group hosts no permanent civilian population, only rotating military and scientific personnel?
IOT: Which territory's Diego Garcia atoll hosts a major joint military base and has no permanent civilian residents?
IRL: Which country is nicknamed the 'Emerald Isle' for its intensely green landscape, a product of near-constant Atlantic rainfall?
IRN: Which country is home to one of the oldest continuous civilizations on Earth, with the Persian Empire tracing back over 2,500 years?
IRQ: Which country sits on the land historically known as Mesopotamia, widely considered the cradle of writing, the wheel, and humanity's earliest cities?
ISL: Which country has no standing army and no mosquitoes, one of the only places on Earth confirmed to be mosquito-free?
ISR: Which country borders the Dead Sea, which sits at roughly 430 meters below sea level, the lowest land elevation on Earth?
ITA: Which country has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country in the world?
JAM: Which country was the birthplace of reggae music and produced two of the fastest sprint times ever recorded, both by the same sprinter?
JEY: Which island's tidal range is among the largest in the world, with the sea receding far enough at low tide to walk to neighboring islets?
JOR: Which country's ancient city of Petra was carved directly into rose-colored sandstone cliffs and remained largely unknown to the Western world until 1812?
JPN: Which country's Geospatial Information Authority recounted its islands using modern digital mapping in 2023 and found 14,125 of them — more than double the previously cited figure of 6,800?
KAZ: Which is the world's largest landlocked country by land area, roughly the size of Western Europe?
KEN: Which country's Great Rift Valley is part of a tectonic split so large it's slowly pulling East Africa apart, potentially forming a new ocean over millions of years?
KGZ: Which country is landlocked and more than 90% mountainous, one of the most vertical countries on Earth?
KHM: Which country's Angkor Wat is the largest religious monument in the world by land area, originally built as a Hindu temple before becoming Buddhist?
KIR: Which is the only country in the world situated in all four hemispheres — north, south, east, and west?
KNA: Which is the smallest sovereign country in the Americas, both by area and by population?
KOR: Which country has one of the fastest average internet speeds in the world, a legacy of aggressive early broadband investment in the late 1990s?
KOSOVO: Which territory declared independence from Serbia in 2008 and is recognized by roughly half of UN member states, making its sovereignty one of the most contested in the world?
KWT: Which country has one of the highest concentrations of proven oil reserves in the world relative to its small land area?
LAO: Which is the only landlocked country in Southeast Asia, with parts of it still littered with unexploded ordnance from the Vietnam War era?
LBN: Which country is one of the only places where you can ski in the mountains and swim in the Mediterranean on the same day?
LBR: Which country was founded in the 19th century by freed American slaves and modeled its flag and constitution closely on those of the United States?
LBY: Which country holds Africa's largest proven oil reserves, despite the Sahara Desert covering roughly 90% of its territory?
LCA: Which is the only country in the world named after a woman — Saint Lucy of Syracuse?
LIE: Which is one of only two doubly landlocked countries in the world, meaning it's surrounded entirely by other landlocked countries?
LKA: Which country was the first in the world to elect a female head of government, Sirimavo Bandaranaike, in 1960?
LSO: Which is the only country in the world located entirely above 1,000 meters in elevation, earning it the nickname 'Kingdom in the Sky'?
LTU: Which country was once the largest in Europe, when its Grand Duchy stretched from the Baltic Sea nearly to the Black Sea in the 15th century?
LUX: Which country has one of the highest GDP-per-capita figures in the world, and has offered free public transport across the entire country since 2020?
LVA: Which country's capital has one of the largest and best-preserved collections of Art Nouveau buildings in Europe?
MAC: Which territory generates more gambling revenue than Las Vegas, making it the largest gambling market in the world?
MAF: Which island is split between France and the Netherlands, one of the smallest landmasses in the world shared by two sovereign nations?
MAR: Almost none of the classic film 'Casablanca' was actually filmed in which country's real city of the same name?
MCO: Which is the second-smallest country in the world, yet more densely populated than any other sovereign nation?
MDA: Which country's Milestii Mici wine cellar holds the largest wine collection in the world by bottle count, stretching over 200 km of underground tunnels?
MDG: Which island split from mainland Africa roughly 150 million years ago, leaving over 90% of its wildlife found nowhere else on Earth?
MDV: Which is the lowest-lying country in the world, with an average ground elevation of just 1.5 meters above sea level?
MEX: Chocolate, corn, tomatoes, and chili peppers all spread to the rest of the world from which country following European contact?
MHL: Which country's Bikini Atoll was the site of 23 nuclear tests between 1946 and 1958, and remains uninhabited by its original islanders to this day?
MKD: Which country changed its name from simply 'Macedonia' in 2019 to resolve a decades-long naming dispute with its neighbor to the south?
MLI: Which country's ancient city of Timbuktu was once one of the wealthiest trading centers in the world, famed for gold, salt, and a legendary library of manuscripts?
MLT: Which country's capital is so densely packed with historic buildings that the entire city itself is listed as a single UNESCO World Heritage Site?
MMR: Which country was known as Burma until a military government officially renamed it in 1989, a change still not recognized by every country?
MNE: Which country's name literally means 'Black Mountain,' referencing the dark, forested peaks that dominate its coastline?
MNG: Which is the least densely populated sovereign country on Earth, where roughly a third of the population still lives a nomadic or semi-nomadic herding lifestyle?
MNP: The Mariana Trench, home to the Challenger Deep — the deepest known point in all of Earth's oceans — lies near which territory?
MOZ: Which country's coastline stretches for roughly 2,500 km along the Indian Ocean, among the longest of any African country?
MRT: Which was the last country in the world to formally criminalize slavery, doing so in law only in 1981 and adding criminal penalties in 2007?
MSR: Which territory's capital was buried by volcanic ash after the Soufriere Hills eruptions in the 1990s and remains an abandoned exclusion zone today?
MUS: Which island was the only known home of the dodo bird, which went extinct within roughly a century of the island's discovery by sailors?
MWI: A lake in which country holds more fish species than any other lake in the world, with estimates exceeding 1,000 distinct species?
MYS: Which country's Petronas Towers were the tallest buildings in the world when completed in 1998, and remain the tallest twin towers on Earth?
N-CYPRUS: Which territory declared independence in 1983 and is recognized by only one UN member state, making it one of the most diplomatically isolated territories on Earth?
NAM: Which was the first country in the world to write environmental protection directly into its constitution, in 1990?
NCL: Which territory's lagoon is one of the largest in the world and a UNESCO World Heritage Site, home to an extraordinary diversity of marine life?
NER: Which country is named for the river running through it, even though roughly 80% of its territory lies within the Sahara Desert?
NFK: Which island was originally settled to grow flax and pine for the British Navy, then later used as a particularly remote penal colony?
NGA: Which country's Nollywood film industry has for years ranked among the two or three largest in the world by number of films produced annually, often surpassing Hollywood in sheer volume?
NIC: Which country's largest lake is one of the only freshwater lakes in the world known to contain bull sharks, which adapted to survive without salt water?
NIU: Which is one of the world's largest coral islands, with a population small enough that nearly everyone knows each other by name?
NLD: About a quarter of which country sits below sea level, protected by an extensive system of dikes, dunes, and pumps?
NOR: Which country's coastline, including all its fjords and islands, stretches over 100,000 km — long enough to circle the Earth roughly two and a half times?
NPL: Which is the only country in the world whose national flag isn't rectangular, made instead of two stacked triangular pennants?
NRU: Which is the smallest island nation in the world, and, for a period in the 1980s, had one of the highest per-capita incomes on Earth from phosphate mining?
NZL: Which was the first country in the world to grant women the right to vote in parliamentary elections, in 1893?
OMN: Which country's frankincense trade helped make the region one of the ancient world's most valuable trade routes, with the resin once worth its weight in gold?
PAK: Which country is home to K2, the second-highest mountain on Earth, and more peaks over 8,000 meters than any country besides Nepal and China?
PAN: The canal in which country is one of the only places in the world where ships heading from the Atlantic to the Pacific effectively travel from northwest to southeast?
PCN: Which territory has one of the smallest populations of any in the world, descended largely from the mutineers of HMS Bounty and their Tahitian companions?
PER: Which country's Machu Picchu sits at over 2,400 meters and was unknown to the wider world until its 1911 rediscovery by American historian Hiram Bingham?
PHL: Which country is made up of 7,641 islands, officially counted by the national mapping agency (NAMRIA) in 2016 — many of the smallest still uninhabited and unnamed?
PLW: Which country's Jellyfish Lake is home to millions of jellyfish that have lost their sting over thousands of years of evolving without natural predators?
PNG: Which is one of the most linguistically diverse places on Earth, with over 800 distinct languages spoken?
POL: Which country's Wieliczka Salt Mine has been continuously mined since the 13th century and contains entire chapels carved out of rock salt?
PRI: Which territory was once home to the Arecibo Observatory, for decades one of the largest radio telescopes ever built and used in the search for extraterrestrial signals?
PRK: Which country and its neighbor to the south remain technically at war, since the 1953 war between them ended with an armistice, not a formal peace treaty?
PRT: Which country's Age of Exploration in the 15th century helped launch the first truly global trade networks, connecting Europe, Africa, Asia, and the Americas?
PRY: Which country generates nearly all of its domestic electricity from hydropower and is a net exporter of electricity thanks to the massive Itaipu Dam?
PSE: Which territory is recognized by a majority of United Nations member states, though it holds only non-member observer status at the UN itself?
PYF: Which territory's most famous island is credited as the birthplace of the overwater bungalow, a hospitality concept believed to have originated there in the 1960s?
QAT: Which country has one of the highest GDP-per-capita figures in the world, built largely on its vast natural gas reserves?
ROU: Which country's Sighișoara, in Transylvania, is the birthplace of Vlad the Impaler — the 15th-century Wallachian ruler whose brutal reputation inspired Dracula?
RUS: Which country spans eleven time zones, more than any other in the world?
RWA: Which country banned single-use plastic bags nationwide in 2008, well before most of the world began considering similar policies?
SAU: Which country has no permanent rivers anywhere within it, one of the largest nations on Earth without a single one?
SDN: Which country has more pyramids than Egypt, with over 200 ancient Nubian pyramids scattered across its desert?
SEN: Which country's Lake Retba, also called Lac Rose, turns a distinctive pink color due to algae that thrive in its extremely salty water?
SGP: Which is one of only three surviving city-states in the world, alongside Monaco and Vatican City?
SGS: Which territory has no permanent civilian population but hosts more breeding king penguins than almost anywhere else on Earth?
SHN: Which is one of the most remote inhabited islands in the world, and was the site of Napoleon Bonaparte's final exile until his death in 1821?
SIACHEN-GLACIER: Which glacier, disputed between India and Pakistan, is the highest battlefield on Earth, with troops stationed at altitudes above 6,000 meters?
SLB: Which country was the site of some of the fiercest Pacific battles of World War II, including the pivotal Battle of Guadalcanal?
SLE: Which country's capital was founded in 1792 as a settlement for freed and formerly enslaved people, which is how the city got its name?
SLV: Which country became the first in the world to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender, in 2021?
SMR: Which country claims to be the oldest surviving sovereign state and constitutional republic in the world, founded, by tradition, in the year 301 AD?
SOM: Which country has the longest coastline of any country on mainland Africa?
SOMALILAND: Which territory declared independence from Somalia in 1991 and functions as a de facto independent state, though no UN member country formally recognizes it?
SPM: Which territory is the last remaining piece of the once-vast territory of New France still governed by France, just off the coast of Canada?
SRB: Which country's capital has reportedly been destroyed and rebuilt around 40 times throughout its history, owing to its position at the confluence of two major rivers — one of the most fought-over city sites in Europe?
SSD: Which country became the world's newest in 2011, seceding from Sudan after decades of civil war?
STP: Which is Africa's second-smallest country, its economy historically built almost entirely on cocoa production?
SUR: Which is the smallest sovereign country in South America, with over 90% of its territory remaining covered in pristine rainforest?
SVK: Which country is home to over 100 castles and around 400 chateaux and manor houses — one of the highest concentrations of historic fortifications in Europe, packed into a country roughly the size of Vermont and New Hampshire combined?
SVN: Which country's capital was named European Green Capital in 2016, with more than half the city's area given over to green space?
SWE: Which country's legal tradition of Allemansratten, the 'right to roam,' lets anyone walk, camp, or forage on almost any land, public or private?
SWZ: Which country changed its name in 2018, when its king officially renamed it to mark 50 years of independence?
SXM: Which island is famous for a beach right next to the runway, where jets land so low overhead that tourists gather just to feel the engine blast?
SYC: Which country is home to the coco de mer, a palm tree that produces the largest and heaviest seed of any plant on Earth?
SYR: Which country's capital is considered one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world, with habitation dating back thousands of years?
TCA: Which territory is home to one of the largest barrier reef systems in the world, drawing divers from across the globe?
TCD: Which country's namesake lake has shrunk by roughly 90% since the 1960s, once one of the largest lakes in Africa and now a fraction of its former size?
TGO: Which is one of the narrowest countries in Africa, stretching just over 50 km wide at its narrowest point?
THA: Which is the only country in Southeast Asia never colonized by a European power?
TJK: Which is one of the most mountainous countries in the world, with over 90% of its territory covered by mountains?
TKM: Which country's Darvaza gas crater, nicknamed the 'Door to Hell,' has been burning in the Karakum Desert for decades — locals still debate exactly when and how it was first ignited?
TLS: Which country became Asia's newest in 2002, after gaining full independence following decades of Indonesian and earlier Portuguese rule?
TON: Which is the only Pacific island nation never formally colonized by a foreign power, its monarchy tracing back over a thousand years through several ruling dynasties even though the current House of Tupou has reigned only since 1875?
TTO: Which country is the birthplace of steelpan music, invented from repurposed oil drums in the 20th century?
TUN: Which country's desert town of Matmata, with its underground cave dwellings, was used as a filming location for Luke Skywalker's home on Tatooine in Star Wars?
TUR: Which country's largest city straddles two continents, split by the Bosphorus Strait between Europe and Asia?
TWN: Which economy produces the vast majority of the world's most advanced computer chips, largely through a single company, TSMC?
TZA: Which country is home to both the highest point in Africa, Mount Kilimanjaro, and one of the continent's lowest points, the floor of Lake Tanganyika?
UGA: Which country is home to more than half of the world's remaining mountain gorillas, primarily in its Bwindi Impenetrable Forest?
UKR: Which country's extraordinarily fertile black soil, called chernozem, made it historically known as the 'breadbasket of Europe'?
URY: Which was the first country in the world to fully legalize the production, sale, and use of recreational cannabis nationwide, in 2013?
USA: Which country has no official national language at the federal level, despite English being the de facto language of government and commerce?
UZB: Which is one of only two doubly landlocked countries in the world, meaning every country that borders it is also landlocked?
VAT: Which is the smallest sovereign state in the world, with a population under 1,000 and its own train station, postal system, and currency?
VCT: Which country's Wallilabou Bay was a filming location for Pirates of the Caribbean, its film-set dock structures still standing today?
VEN: Which country's Angel Falls is the tallest uninterrupted waterfall in the world, dropping nearly 980 meters from the top of a flat-topped mountain called a tepui?
VGB: Which territory has more registered offshore companies than people, a legacy of its role as a major international financial center?
VIR: Which U.S. territory drives on the left side of the road, despite nearly everywhere else in the country driving on the right?
VNM: Which is the world's second-largest coffee exporter, behind only Brazil, thanks to French colonial-era plantations that took root in its highlands?
VUT: Which country's Mount Yasur is one of the most accessible active volcanoes on Earth, letting visitors stand near its erupting crater rim?
WLF: Which is one of the least-visited territories on Earth, having lacked an airport capable of handling large international flights until relatively recently?
WSM: Which country skipped December 30, 2011 entirely, jumping forward a full day to switch sides of the International Date Line and align with its main trading partners?
YEM: Which country's Socotra archipelago is home to the alien-looking dragon's blood tree, found nowhere else on Earth and named for its deep red sap?
ZAF: Which country has three capital cities — Pretoria, Cape Town, and Bloemfontein — each hosting a different branch of government?
ZMB: Victoria Falls, shared with a neighboring country and classified as the largest sheet of falling water in the world by combined height and width, straddles the border of which country?
ZWE: Which country takes its name from a medieval stone city whose ruins are the largest ancient stone structure in sub-Saharan Africa, built entirely without mortar?
